channel.title: NPR Topics: Business
channel.description: Find the latest business news with reports on Wall Street, interest rates, banking, companies, and U.S. and world financial markets. Subscribe to the Business Story of the Day podcast.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Foreign ticket holders from World Cup teams' countries won't have to pay bonds to enter U.S.
item.description: The Trump administration is suspending a requirement that visitors from countries that have qualified for the World Cup and bought tickets for the tournament pay as much as $15,000 in bonds to enter the U.S.
item.content:encoded:
The Trump administration is suspending a requirement that visitors from countries that have qualified for the World Cup and bought tickets for the tournament pay as much as $15,000 in bonds to enter the U.S.

(Image credit: Mark Schiefelbein)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Senate confirms Kevin Warsh as next chair of the Federal Reserve
item.description: Warsh has argued there's room for the central bank to lower interest rates, but that could be challenging at a time of rising inflation.
item.content:encoded:
Warsh has argued there's room for the central bank to lower interest rates, but that could be challenging at a time of rising inflation.

(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Short flights are popular. Will they last?
item.description: Some flights are less than 100 miles, and they're the backbone of the U.S. air system, linking small towns and big cities. But short routes were in decline even before jet fuel costs spiked.
item.content:encoded:
Some flights are less than 100 miles, and they're the backbone of the U.S. air system, linking small towns and big cities. But short routes were in decline even before jet fuel costs spiked.

(Image credit: Charles Krupa)
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell

item.title: A tale of two manufacturers: How two companies are diversifying their supply chains
item.description: After weathering Trump's tariffs, one U.S. and one Chinese manufacturer are looking to further diversify their supply chains, even as Beijing and Washington try to stabilize ties.
item.content:encoded: After weathering Trump's tariffs, one U.S. and one Chinese manufacturer are looking to further diversify their supply chains, even as Beijing and Washington try to stabilize ties.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Pak

item.title: OpenAI's Sam Altman takes the stand to fend off Elon Musk's accusations he 'stole a charity'
item.description: The trial pits two of tech's most famous personalities against each other and could lead to seismic changes for the maker of ChatGPT.
item.content:encoded:
The trial pits two of tech's most famous personalities against each other and could lead to seismic changes for the maker of ChatGPT.

(Image credit: JOSH EDELSON)
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: Farmers want firm trade deal from Trump's China visit
item.description: President Trump's state visit to China will be closely watched in the American heartland, where his trade war has resulted in grain farmers losing considerable business from international buyers.
item.content:encoded: President Trump's state visit to China will be closely watched in the American heartland, where his trade war has resulted in grain farmers losing considerable business from international buyers.
item.dc:creator: Kirk Siegler

item.title: Federal Reserve Bank of Chicago CEO on inflation and latest consumer price index
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin asks Austan Goolsbee, president and CEO of the Federal Reserve Bank of Chicago, about rising inflation and the latest federal report on consumer prices.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Michel Martin asks Austan Goolsbee, president and CEO of the Federal Reserve Bank of Chicago, about rising inflation and the latest federal report on consumer prices.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Transportation Secretary Duffy filmed a reality show, funded by firms he regulates
item.description: Secretary Duffy, his wife and nine kids took a "Great American Road Trip" to celebrate America's 250th. They say no taxpayers funded the project. But a nonprofit with transport-related sponsors did.
item.content:encoded: Secretary Duffy, his wife and nine kids took a "Great American Road Trip" to celebrate America's 250th. They say no taxpayers funded the project. But a nonprofit with transport-related sponsors did.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Inflation jumps to its highest level since 2023. Here are 3 things costing a lot more
item.description: Rising gasoline prices pushed inflation to its highest level in almost three years in April. Consumer prices were up 3.8% from a year ago.
item.content:encoded:
Rising gasoline prices pushed inflation to its highest level in almost three years in April. Consumer prices were up 3.8% from a year ago.

(Image credit: Ronaldo Schemidt)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: The economic chilling effect of Trump's immigration crackdown
item.description: New research finds that ICE raids and deportation fears disrupted local economies, reduced work among undocumented immigrants, and may have hurt some U.S.-born workers too.
item.content:encoded:
New research finds that ICE raids and deportation fears disrupted local economies, reduced work among undocumented immigrants, and may have hurt some U.S.-born workers too.

(Image credit: Stephen Maturen)
item.dc:creator: Greg Rosalsky
